mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-trading-day window.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration. This joint expansion compresses risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or partial correlation with INDPRO alone explains over 90% of the variance.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of tightening episodes, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS changes becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) during rate-hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings and consumption expenditures reflect strengthening aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no monotonic relationship with RSAFS growth rates, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its 12-month moving average by more than 2.0 percent, supply chain bottlenecks ease and capacity utilization rises, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are flat or negative when INDPRO breaches the threshold, or the signal generates more false positives than actual trend reversals over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment indicates a self-reinforcing labor demand shock. This dual improvement sustains earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for GDP growth, or 60-day forward returns under the joint signal do not exceed the single PAYEMS signal by at least 10 basis points per month.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery environments, a sharp rebound in housing starts amplifies construction-related demand and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-trading-day window.
failure_condition: HOUST rebounds fail to translate into positive equity returns during recovery periods, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 50% when tested against a random baseline over a 2-year window.
